sections.paragraph: ἀπάτη, ης, ἡ [orig. uncertain, akin to ἀπατάω] ‘deception as quality or instance’, deception, trick, fraud, sham Col 2:8; Eph 4:22 (‘deceitful desires’ = passions fostered by deception); ἐν πάσῃ ἀπάτῃ w. every kind of wicked deception 2 Th 2:10; Hb 3:13. In Mt 13:22; Mk 4:19 ἀ. apparently signifies a false sense of pleas-urable security seductively generated by possessions; a similar interpretation has been suggested for 2 Pt 2:13, but this pass. may well refer to deceptions that their sponsors perpetrate while they socialize with unwary fellow members.
